title: The game that makes me actually want to exercise
summary: Pedometer++ 8.0 is a game that gamifies daily exercise, successfully motivating users to take more walks. The app merges entertainment mechanics with fitness tracking to make physical activity more engaging. This demonstrates how game design can serve as an effective tool for promoting healthy habits and wellness.
content:
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 127, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, my Scorpion challenges your Sub-Zero to a duel, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) 
This week, I've been reading about Hasan Piker and lines and David Sacks and sleep learning, catching up on Andor (for me) and Young Jedi Adventures (for my toddler), poring over thousands of new Artemis II photos, listening to many hours of possibly the longest YouTube video ever, taking more walks thanks to Pedometer++ 8.0, losing my mind during every second of every Arsenal match, and spendi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 让我真正想要运动的游戏
zh.summary: Pedometer++ 8.0是一款将日常锻炼游戏化的应用，成功激励用户增加散步次数。该应用将娱乐机制与健身追踪相结合，使体育运动更具吸引力。这表明游戏设计可以成为促进健康习惯和健康生活的有效工具。

title: Quantum dot TVs beat RGB LED TVs, says the company that makes QDs for TVs
summary: Quantum-dot TVs continue to offer superior image quality compared to RGB LED panels, according to manufacturer Nanosys's demonstration at Display Week 2026. While RGB LED dominates as the year's hottest TV trend, the side-by-side comparison revealed potential limitations of RGB LED backlights versus super quantum dot technology. The findings suggest quantum-dot TVs remain a compelling alternative despite RGB LED's market momentum.
content:
RGB LED technology might be everyone’s focus for TV tech in 2026, but quantum-dot TVs still might be the better choice. | Photo by John Higgins / The Verge	

At the Los Angeles Convention Center, two 85-inch TVs sat side by side inside the Nanosys meeting room at Display Week - a yearly business-to-business convention focusing on the technology that goes into displays of all types. One TV was a mini-LED panel with super quantum dots, and the other was an RGB LED - this year's hottest TV trend. Both TVs were showing the same content at the same time to highlight the differences between the two technologies - or more specifically, to show the potential failings of RGB LED backlights when compared to super quantum dot (SQD), which uses blue LEDs for the backlight.
I should probably mention that Nan …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 量子点电视击败RGB LED电视，量子点制造商表示
zh.summary: 根据量子点制造商Nanosys在2026年显示周的演示，量子点电视相比RGB LED面板仍然提供更优越的图像质量。尽管RGB LED作为今年最热门的电视趋势主导市场，但并排比较揭示了RGB LED背光相比超级量子点技术的潜在局限。研究结果表明，尽管RGB LED具有市场势头，量子点电视仍然是一个有竞争力的替代方案。
